decision:0.text: R1.1 ledger-verb consolidation decision, 2026-07-19, owner 0xkhdr. Blanket consolidation under a single `specd record <kind>` verb is REJECTED. The T1 audit found only 4 of the 14 audited verbs are thin append-a-record shells (recurring, spike, unlink, decision); the other 10 carry real logic - incident scaffolds a successor spec with typed program links, midreq synthesizes an invalidating Amendment, release computes evidence-set and bootstrap digests, deploy validates against the frozen candidate, drift is a read-only projection not an append, eval guards path traversal, exception enforces a constitutional non-waivable guard, memory does cross-spec threshold promotion, link does cycle-refusing slug validation, handshake builds the driver guide. Consolidating those behind one verb would hide distinct authority and validation semantics behind a kind string, so it is not done.

decision:1.text: R1.1 consolidation decision part 2 of 4 - KEPT WITH NAMED CONSUMERS (R1.1 edge case), 2026-07-19, owner 0xkhdr: incident (scripts/regress-domains.sh:196,201; maintenance/incident.md template), release (internal/cmd/deploy.go:33 refuses deploy without a frozen candidate), deploy (internal/cmd/report.go:452,513,574; internal/core/adapter_envelope.go:178), midreq (internal/core/freshness.go:62; internal/core/report.go:168), drift (MCP surface via internal/core/manifest_tools.go:79), eval (scripts/regress-domains.sh:105), exception (internal/core/gates/security/allowlist.go:29), memory (embed_templates/steering/memory.md:11; workflow.md:26), link and unlink (program.json graph read by internal/cmd/lifecycle.go:121, report.go:502, archive.go:30), decision (internal/cmd/report.go:222; internal/orchestration/sense.go:31; mandated by embed_templates/roles/craftsman.md:17), handshake (embed_templates/AGENTS.md:7 agent boot; scripts/release-smoke.sh:39).

decision:2.text: R1.1 consolidation decision part 3 of 4 - DEFERRED FOR DELETION, dated 2026-07-19, revisit 2026-10-19, owner 0xkhdr: `specd recurring` and `specd spike` are write-only - recurring-results.jsonl has no non-test reader and PlanRecurringSuccessor is never called outside tests; State.Spikes() is read only by state.go own re-parse. Both are deferred, not deleted here, because deletion is a follow-up spec per this spec non-goals.

decision:3.text: R1.1 consolidation decision part 4 of 4 - FOLLOW-UP DEFECT recorded 2026-07-19, owner 0xkhdr: `specd decision` writes state.Records while the governance store core.DecisionPath (.specd/specs/<slug>/decisions.json, read by internal/cmd/drift.go:23 and internal/context/decision.go:30) has no non-test writer - the two decision stores are unrelated and the governance one is hand-authored. Resolving that split is a follow-up spec.

decision:4.text: R2.1/R2.2 metrics export format: KEEP Prometheus text exposition (report --format prometheus) as the single supported metrics export; DEPRECATE report --format otel and schedule it for deletion in a follow-up spec (deletion is a non-goal of this spec). Evidence: internal/adapter/otel_export.go is reached only from internal/cmd/report_otel.go; every other otel mention in the tree is its own tests (report_otel_test.go, otel_export_test.go), the command palette enum in internal/core/commands.go, or generated docs/command-reference.md. No driver in scripts/, .github/, or embed_templates/. It is also redundant with the documented integration path: docs/adapter-contract.md and docs/adapters/telemetry.md specify OTel export as an EXTERNAL adapter concern mapping the neutral event/v1 JSONL stream, so the built-in span projection is a duplicate schema with no reader. Prometheus is kept because node_exporter textfile output is a file drop matching specd zero-runtime-dependency no-daemon posture, its metric names are a documented API contract, and it carries the worker tokens/cost/duration counter series. Correction to the framing: scripts/regress-domains.sh:204 is an enum-rejection probe using --format __bogus__, not a live prometheus consumer; the keep rests on the documented metric-name API and posture fit, not on that probe. --format event is a third format, out of R2.1 scope, unaffected and retained as the neutral adapter stream; it has no script/CI driver but is the documented adapter integration surface. Documented in docs/observability.md.

decision:5.text: T4 verify passes and all six contract docs carry cited Driver headers, but brain report fails scope: docs/observability.md was already modified-uncommitted in the tree when the T4 lease was claimed (leftover from the prior --format otel deprecation task). Not reverting or committing another task's work under the T4 lease; escalating to Brain to commit or land that change first, then re-report T4.

decision:6.text: CORRECTION 2026-07-19 to the decision-store finding recorded earlier in this spec (see decision:3). That finding claimed `specd decision` writing state.Records while core.DecisionPath (.specd/specs/<slug>/decisions.json) has no non-test writer is a defect requiring a follow-up spec. It is NOT a defect and no follow-up spec is warranted. The two stores are intentionally different: `specd decision --text` appends a narrative record (Kind/Text/Scope) to state.Records, consumed by report --history (internal/cmd/report.go:222) and internal/orchestration/sense.go:31; decisions.json holds structured governance declarations (core.DecisionV1: ID, Status, Owner, CreatedAt, ReviewAt, ExpiresAt, Supersedes, AffectedInvariants) consumed by drift, the governance gate and context assembly. decisions.json is a DECLARED-INPUT file, opt-in by presence, not a CLI output: core.LoadDecisions documents "Missing file means governance is unconfigured"; internal/cmd/registry.go:216 arms the gate only via GovernanceRequired = len(decisions) > 0 || len(exceptions) > 0; and the drift verb is specified in internal/core/commands.go:321 as projecting declared invariants and active decisions "without writing". Decisive evidence: drift.json (the invariants half of the same model, core.DriftPath) likewise has no writer and is not considered defective. Adding a CLI writer would force ID/Status/Owner/ReviewAt/ExpiresAt flags, inventing values for fields whose purpose is deliberate human authorship. ROOT CAUSE of the bad finding: it was inferred from grep coverage (no non-test writer implies broken) without reading the load-function contracts. RESIDUAL REAL GAP, narrower: no file under docs/, internal/core/embed_templates/ or AGENTS.md mentions decisions.json or drift.json, so the governance and drift surfaces are undiscoverable — docs/validation-gates.md:59 calls the governance gate opt-in without stating which file to author or in what shape. That is a documentation fix, not a spec.
